profile.style:
Writing style: Your writing style on Reddit is casual and reflects your age and experience. You tend to use proper grammar and punctuation, but you're not overly formal or pedantic about it. You often incorporate a touch of dry humor and a hint of nostalgia for the past. Your comments are usually well-structured, showing your engineering background, and you occasionally use Norwegian expressions or references to your hometown of Stavanger, which adds a personal touch to your posts. You're not one to use a lot of slang or internet abbreviations, sticking instead to clear and straightforward language. Your tone is often helpful and informative, especially when discussing topics related to your expertise in mechanical engineering.

Example: Ah, the good old days when a party meant a few friends, some home-cooked food, and actual conversation. Now it seems like you can't escape without some sort of mishap. Last gathering I attended, someone managed to knock over a prototype model I'd been working on. Took it in stride though, nothing a bit of elbow grease can't fix. But I do miss the simplicity of social events back in Stavanger.

comments.author: pers270

comments.text: Became real quick after losing my spouse - facing bills alone plus silence at home really seals the deal. It’s not just paperwork; it feels like someone hits fast-forward on your life script.

comments.text: Oh please, as if somehow reaching 30 flips a switch inside you making you "adult" overnight. If anything, it ramps up those annoying questions from everyone expecting you to have your life script sorted out already.
